question_text: Which state of matter can be compressed easily and why?

options.label: A
options.text: Solid, because particles are close together with space between them

options.label: B
options.text: Liquid, because it has no definite shape

options.label: C
options.text: Gas, because particles are far apart with large spaces between them

options.label: D
options.text: All states of matter compress equally easily

correct_answer: Gas, because particles are far apart with large spaces between them
explanation: Gases are easily compressed because their particles are spread far apart with lots of empty space between them. When you apply pressure to a gas, the particles can move closer together to fill that empty space. In contrast, solids and liquids have particles that are already tightly packed, so they are very difficult to compress.
